data.title: Nasdaq 100's Remarkable Performance Attributed to Steve Jobs' Influence
data.description:
Eric Balchunas, senior ETF Analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, posted on X. The Nasdaq 100 index has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 since the launch of the QQQ ETF in 1999, doubling its returns and surpassing the performance of every active fund manager. The key to its success, according to Balchunas, can be traced back to two words: Steve Jobs. 

In 1980, Steve Jobs made the pivotal decision to list Apple on the Nasdaq, which was then considered an upstart third-place exchange. This move positioned Nasdaq as the hub for tech visionaries, attracting top U.S. innovators. Jobs' influence was further solidified during his comeback in the 2000s, enhancing Nasdaq's reputation as the go-to exchange for leading tech companies.

Balchunas compares this strategic move to Michael Jordan's choice of Nike over Adidas and Converse in 1983, which subsequently attracted top athletes to the brand. Balchunas expressed interest in creating a documentary about this transformative period and is seeking the individual who took Jobs' call in 1980 to discuss Apple's listing on Nasdaq.

data.title: Japan's FSA Recognizes XRP and Over 100 Crypto Assets for Trading
data.description: Japan's Financial Services Agency (FSA) has officially recognized XRP and more than 100 other crypto assets as eligible for trading on registered exchanges in the country. According to NS3.AI, this recognition is part of the FSA's framework for assets that comply with the Payment Services Act. However, the agency clarified that this acknowledgment does not serve as an endorsement of the assets.

data.title: BlackRock Eyes U.S. ETF Market with New Nasdaq 100 Tracking Fund
data.description:
BlackRock is setting its sights on a segment of the $13.7 trillion U.S. ETF market, traditionally dominated by Invesco, by introducing a fund that tracks the Nasdaq 100 Index. According to BlockBeats, documents filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Monday reveal that the iShares Nasdaq 100 ETF will track this index under the trading code IQQ. If launched, IQQ will be among the few U.S.-listed ETFs specifically tracking the Nasdaq 100 Index and the first of its kind not managed by Invesco.

Since the Nasdaq Exchange introduced the index in 1985, it has been cautious in licensing its use. While there are ETFs in the U.S. market that overlay Nasdaq 100 components through derivatives, Invesco has nearly monopolized the market for pure Nasdaq 100 Index ETFs.

This collaboration has led to the creation of the $374 billion Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 (trading code QQQ), one of the largest ETFs globally, and the $70 billion Invesco Nasdaq 100 ETF.

data.title: Quantum Computing's Impact on Bitcoin Mining Assessed by BTQ Technologies
data.description:
BTQ Technologies has released a research paper titled 'Kardashev Scale Quantum Computing for Bitcoin Mining,' which provides a quantitative evaluation of the physical costs associated with using quantum computing for Bitcoin mining. According to ChainCatcher, the study highlights a common market confusion between two types of quantum threats: attacks on Bitcoin's elliptic curve digital signatures, which are considered real and urgent, and quantum mining accelerated by Grover's algorithm, which is theoretically possible but extremely costly in practice.

The paper argues that to significantly impact consensus, a quantum computing cluster with energy consumption far exceeding current human civilization levels would be required. The research introduces an open-source resource estimation model that includes key components such as reversible double SHA-256 computation, fault-tolerant quantum error correction, and large-scale qubit scheduling. The conclusion emphasizes that the more immediate risk to Bitcoin arises from vulnerabilities in cryptographic signatures rather than quantum mining capabilities.

data.title: Strategy added 4,871 bitcoins to its holdings last week.
data.description: According to market sources, Strategy disclosed that it increased its holdings by 4,871 Bitcoins last week, at a total cost of approximately $329.9 million. As of April 5, 2026, Strategy held a total of 766,970 BTC, with a total holding cost of approximately $58.02 billion and an average price of approximately $75,644 per BTC.

data.title: BlackRock plans to launch a Nasdaq 100 ETF to challenge Invesco's monopoly.
data.description: BlackRock is eyeing a segment of the $13.7 trillion U.S. ETF market long dominated by Invesco: tracking the Nasdaq 100 index. According to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Monday, the iShares Nasdaq 100 ETF, trading under the ticker symbol IQQ, will track this index. If launched, IQQ will be one of the few U.S.-listed ETFs solely tracking the Nasdaq 100, and the first of its kind not managed by Invesco. The Nasdaq exchange has been cautious in its licensing of the index since its inception in 1985. While ETFs that incorporate Nasdaq 100 constituent stocks through derivatives exist in the U.S. market, Invesco has virtually monopolized the pure Nasdaq 100 ETF market. This partnership gave rise to the Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 (ticker symbol QQQ), a $374 billion ETF, one of the world's largest ETFs, and the Invesco Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQM), with $70 billion in assets. (Jinshi)

data.title: Robinhood private equity funds rebounded 30%, but will face new challenges from mega-IPOs like SpaceX.
data.description: Robinhood's private market fund, Robinhood Ventures I, has rebounded by about 30% after an initial lackluster performance. Launched in early March, the fund initially fell 16% on its first day but has since gradually recovered, aiming to provide retail investors with a channel to participate in late-stage unlisted company equity. However, analysts warn that the private market may face short-term volatility pressures as potential IPOs of tech giants like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic approach. Poor performance of leading companies upon listing could drag down secondary market valuations and impact unicorn IPO expectations, including for companies like Stripe. The head of Robinhood Ventures stated that these concerns are largely short-term volatility, and numerous long-term investment opportunities remain. They believe that as AI technology continues to advance, the application ecosystem built around leading models will unleash enormous growth potential. Currently, the fund primarily invests in a few late-stage companies, including fintech companies Airwallex and Stripe, and AI company Databricks, acquiring equity through primary market or licensed secondary market transactions. Furthermore, Robinhood previously attempted to offer European investors exposure to companies like OpenAI and SpaceX through tokenized shares, but this was met with controversy after OpenAI publicly denied any partnership. Currently, Robinhood Ventures is more focused on traditional equity investment pathways, aiming to gain shareholder access to high-quality startups. (Fortune)

data.title: Sharps Technology's annual financial report: SOL holdings exceed 2 million tokens, with approximately 95% in active staking.
data.description: Solana treasury company Sharps Technology released its annual financial results for the year ending December 31, 2025. The report disclosed that the company's SOL token holdings exceeded 2 million, with approximately 95% of SOL tokens actively staked. Total revenue for fiscal year 2025 reached approximately $7 million, including $6.8 million in net staking income. Sharps Technology also stated that total assets at the end of fiscal year 2025 reached $269.1 million, including $250.1 million in digital assets at fair value, $10.4 million in cash, and $14.2 million in positive working capital. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Experts: A temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran faces multiple practical obstacles.
data.description: Li Zixin, assistant researcher at the China Institute of International Studies, stated that while a temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran is not impossible given the current situation, it faces significant difficulties. Even if one is reached, it is more likely to be a stopgap measure than a reliable path to a permanent ceasefire. Firstly, the core demands of both sides are irreconcilable. Iran considers control of the Strait of Hormuz and its stockpile of 60% enriched uranium as its core strategic bargaining chips and has clearly stated that it will not relinquish these fundamental interests for a short-term ceasefire. The US, on the other hand, demands that Iran reopen the Strait and handle its nuclear materials, essentially demanding unilateral concessions from Iran in exchange for short-term actions that would compromise its core interests—a move that touches upon Iran's sovereignty and security bottom line. Secondly, the foundation of trust in the negotiations is extremely weak. While Iran acknowledges exchanging relevant information with the US through friendly countries, it denies engaging in direct negotiations. Meanwhile, US President Trump, while signaling for negotiations, continues to issue military ultimatums, a "fight-and-talk" approach that resembles a strategy of maximum pressure and testing the other side's willingness to compromise, rather than a genuine pursuit of reconciliation. (CCTV News)

data.title: Cybercriminals Exploit Cloud-Based Android Phones to Evade Bank Security
data.description: Cybercriminals are increasingly using rented cloud-based Android phones, which cost between $0.10 and $0.50 per hour, to circumvent bank anti-fraud measures and deplete customer accounts. According to NS3.AI, these attacks continue to depend heavily on social engineering tactics. Malwarebytes researcher Pieter Arntz highlighted that these methods often involve soliciting one-time passwords, login approvals, or transfers to so-called 'safe accounts.'

data.title: Caixin: Some loan facilitation platforms are seeking second growth curves by investing in the cryptocurrency market and expanding overseas.
data.description: With the implementation of new regulations on loan facilitation and a slowdown in consumer credit demand, many consumer loan facilitation platforms have experienced a year-on-year decline in revenue and loan volume, as well as a significant drop in net profit. Some platforms are seeking second growth drivers, such as expanding overseas and investing in the cryptocurrency sector. It is understood that platforms like Lexin and Yirendai have already established overseas operations, with Southeast Asia and Latin America as their main battlegrounds. Weixin Fintech has been continuously investing in the blockchain industry in the past two years, indirectly acquiring a 15% stake in cryptocurrency exchange Thousand Whales Technology (BVI) in 2024, and investing HK$2.395 billion last year in EXIO Group, one of the first batch of licensed virtual asset trading platforms issued by the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission. (Caixin)

data.title: Opinion: Predictive markets become a "real-time radar" for geopolitical risks; bets on the Iran situation dominate Bitcoin volatility.
data.description: Fabian Dori, Chief Investment Officer at Sygnum Bank, analyzed that prediction markets are rapidly evolving into a "real-time radar" for traders monitoring macroeconomic risks during the escalation of the Iranian conflict. Platforms including Polymarket and Kalshi are rapidly repricing their bets on whether the US will escalate the conflict, and this is directly correlated with Bitcoin price movements. Prediction markets price "clear-cut event outcomes" with real money, providing a more differentiated signal for the crypto market, which is significantly driven by "binary events" such as regulation, geopolitics, and protocol escalations. Data shows that prediction market transactions reached approximately 191 million in March, a year-on-year increase of 2838%, with monthly notional trading volume rising to approximately $23.9 billion. Some professional trading teams have incorporated prediction markets into their macroeconomic analysis frameworks, using them alongside indicators such as funding rates, option structures, and fund flows to develop trading strategies before events occur. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Prediction Markets Emerge as Key Tool Amid Iran Conflict
data.description:
Sygnum Bank's Chief Investment Officer, Fabian Dori, has highlighted the evolving role of prediction markets during the escalating Iran conflict. According to Odaily, platforms such as Polymarket and Kalshi are rapidly adjusting the odds on whether the U.S. will escalate the conflict, showing a direct correlation with Bitcoin price movements. These markets use real funds to price 'clear event outcomes,' offering differentiated signals for the crypto market, which is notably influenced by binary events like regulatory changes, geopolitical tensions, and protocol upgrades.

Data indicates that prediction markets recorded approximately 191 million trades in March, marking a 2838% year-on-year increase, with the nominal monthly trading volume rising to about $23.9 billion. Some professional trading teams have integrated prediction markets into their macro analysis frameworks, using them alongside indicators such as funding rates, options structures, and capital flows to develop trading strategies ahead of events.

data.title: Consumer Lending Platforms Explore Overseas Expansion Amid Revenue Decline
data.description: Several consumer lending platforms are experiencing a decline in revenue and loan issuance, driven by new lending regulations and a slowdown in consumer credit demand. According to Odaily, platforms such as Lexin and Yiren Digital have begun expanding their operations overseas, with Southeast Asia and Latin America emerging as key markets. Meanwhile, Vcredit has been focusing on the blockchain industry, having indirectly acquired a 15% stake in the cryptocurrency exchange Thousand Whales Technology (BVI) in 2024. Last year, Vcredit invested HKD 2.395 billion in EXIO Group, one of the first licensed virtual asset trading platforms by the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Alters Claude Subscription Rules, OpenClaw Responds
data.description: Anthropic has revised its subscription rules for Claude, affecting the inclusion of third-party tools like OpenClaw. According to Odaily, users now face additional charges for such integrations. In response, OpenClaw suggested on the X platform that users could switch to using an API Key to avoid billing complexities. Alternatively, they can consider other subscription options such as OpenAI Codex, Qwen, MiniMax, Kimi, and GLM.

data.title: Potential U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Boosts Market Sentiment, Bitcoin and Major Cryptos Rise
data.description:
Market sentiment has improved amid expectations of a potential ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran, leading to increased risk appetite. According to Odaily, the ceasefire plan might take effect on Monday, potentially reopening the Strait of Hormuz. This development has contributed to Bitcoin's rise, strengthening the overall crypto market, with major assets like Ethereum and Solana also experiencing gains. The derivatives market is signaling bullish trends, with improvements in Bitcoin futures structure and a continued decline in 30-day implied volatility. Additionally, Nasdaq 100 futures have risen by 0.8%, reinforcing the risk-on atmosphere.

However, analysts caution that the market rebound remains uncertain. Previous ceasefire announcements have been denied or disproven by Iran, and similar occurrences could lead to a swift market reversal. Furthermore, even if an agreement is reached, its binding effect on Israel remains questionable, potentially limiting the sustainability of risk appetite. Overall, while Bitcoin has upward potential, macroeconomic variables continue to be a significant constraint.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin's rise boosted the overall crypto market, while expectations of a ceasefire in Iran drove risk-on trading.
data.description: Boosted by expectations of a potential ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran, market risk appetite has rebounded. The ceasefire plan is reportedly set to take effect on Monday, potentially reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Bitcoin's rise has driven the overall crypto market higher, with Ethereum, Solana, and other major assets also rising. The derivatives market has also released bullish signals, with Bitcoin futures showing improved structure and 30-day implied volatility continuing to decline. Meanwhile, Nasdaq 100 futures rose 0.8%, reinforcing the risk-on atmosphere. However, analysts point out that the market rebound still faces uncertainty. Recent ceasefire announcements have been repeatedly denied or refuted by Iran; if similar situations occur again, the market could quickly reverse. Furthermore, even if an agreement is reached, its binding force on Israel remains questionable, potentially limiting the sustainability of risk appetite. Overall, while Bitcoin has upside potential, macroeconomic variables remain a key constraint. (CoinDesk) Note: "Risk-on" is a common term in financial markets, meaning the market is in a "risk-averse" state, where investors are willing to take on more risk to pursue returns.

data.title: QCP: Crypto markets downplay escalating Iranian risks, but the sustainability of the latest rally remains to be seen.
data.description: A recent analysis by QCP Capital points out that US President Trump's decision to take action against Iran, postponed again until Tuesday for the fourth time, indicates that the market is gradually becoming immune to the recurring pattern of "tough statements + negotiating signals," cooling expectations of escalating risk. Oil prices weakened, while stock index futures remained stable. In the crypto market, Bitcoin and Ethereum broke through $69,000 and $2,140 respectively during Asian trading hours, triggering approximately $200 million in short liquidations in a illiquid environment. Overall, despite continued geopolitical disturbances, price performance leans more towards stabilization than downward pressure. In terms of funding, institutional funds continue to provide support, with the Bitcoin ETF recording approximately $1.32 billion in net inflows in March. The current market is generally risk-on, with investors not fully prepared for a short-term escalation of conflict. However, with the reopening of US stocks, the sustainability of this rebound remains to be seen.

data.title: OpenClaw responds to Anthropic's subscription rule adjustment: Other subscription services such as API Key or MiniMax can be used.
data.description: In response to Anthropic's adjustment of its Claude subscription rules, which no longer covers third-party tool calls, including OpenClaw, and requires additional payment for such usage paths, OpenClaw issued a statement on the X platform stating that to avoid billing complexity, users can switch to API Keys or consider other subscription options, including OpenAI Codex, Qwen, MiniMax, Kimi, and GLM.

data.title: Planet Evening News
data.description: 1. Iran calls the US 15-point plan "ambitious and illogical"; 2. Santiment: BTC profit/loss ratio reaches 2.95, a short-term price top signal; 3. Spot gold rises above $4,700/oz; 4. Whale yeti.hl deposits 2.19 million USDC into HyperLiquid and buys 58,800 HYPE; 5. BIT: Ethereum call option selling pressure increases, volatility continues to decline; 6. Whale "sets 10 big targets" holding $156 million worth of BTC long positions, currently with a floating profit of $5.52 million; 7. Senior Iranian official: Iran will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for a "temporary ceasefire"; 8. Circle minted approximately 3.25 billion USDC on Solana in the past 7 days, the largest single-week stablecoin minting volume in 2026; 9. Analyst: Reiterates Strategy MSTR buy rating, 2026 target price of $440; 10. The Bank of Japan has sent subtle signals, avoiding a commitment to raise interest rates before its April meeting.
